content.problemStatement:
Problem Statement:**Deleting elements from a 1D array.**.

Deleting elements from a 1D array involves removing an element from a specific position within the array and then shifting the remaining elements to fill the gap.

Here's a step-by-step process for deleting elements from a 1D array:


Determine the Position: Decide which element you want to delete from the array. You need to know the index (position) of the element you want to remove.
Shift Elements: Starting from the position of the element to be deleted, move each element one position to the left until you reach the end of the array. This fills the gap left by the deleted element.
Update Array Size: If your array is adynamically allocated or you're using a mechanism that tracks the size, decrease the size of the array to reflect the new number of elements.
Task aYou are given the code in the IDE. 

Update the code based on the steps given above to get the necessary output!


Input Format
First line contains a single integer N — the size of the array.


Second line contains N space-separated integers.


Third line contains an integer P — the position of the element to delete.



Output Format
If position is valid: Print the updated array after deletion (space-separated).


If position is invalid: Print Invalid Position.
constraints: 1 ≤ N ≤ 100000 (sum of N over all test cases ≤ 100000)
P may be any 32‑bit signed integer
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases.
For each test case:
- A line with integer N, the size of the array.
- A line with N space‑separated integers, the array elements.
- A line with integer P, the 0‑based position of the element to delete.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case output on a separate line:
- If 0 ≤ P < N, print the array after deleting the element at position P, elements separated by a single space. If the resulting array is empty, print an empty line.
- Otherwise print the string "Invalid Position".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Position 2 (0‑based) holds value 30. After removing it the remaining elements are printed.
Position 5 is outside the range [0,2], so the program reports an invalid position.
First test case deletes the first element (1). Second test case deletes the last element (8).

solution.approach: For each test case we read N and the N elements into a vector/array. We then check whether the given position P is within the valid range [0, N‑1]. If it is not, we output "Invalid Position". Otherwise we output all elements except the one at index P, preserving their original order. The operation is O(N) per test case because we may need to traverse the whole array once to print it. No extra data structures are required beyond storing the input array.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the size of the current array. Overall O(total N) ≤ O(100000).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the array of each test case.
